    Galaxy Cluster Substructures

    Cluster Substructures

    Type:Dynamical phenomenon
    Related: Coma Cluster
    Mass Estimation

    Galaxy cluster substructures are dynamically distinct groups, filaments, or infalling systems within or around a cluster [1]. Their presence indicates that a cluster is not fully relaxed, with implications for mass estimates and galaxy evolution [1][2].

    Substructure in the Coma Cluster

    The Coma Cluster was long considered a prototype relaxed system [1], but extensive substructure is now recognized:

    • NGC 4839 group: Identified in X-ray maps [3][4] and galaxy distributions [5], on secondary infall after first apocentre [6][7][8]
    • NGC 4889/4874 merger: Recent or ongoing major merger between the two BCG parent groups [9]
    • Faint subgroups: Many additional faint substructures recently identified [2][10]

    Impact on Analysis

    Substructure contaminates kinematic mass estimates by violating the equilibrium assumption [11]. Velocity distributions of unrelaxed systems depart significantly from Gaussianity [12][13][14]. The Anderson-Darling test quantifies these departures [15]. Post-starburst galaxies in the Coma periphery may be driven by substructure-related interactions [16][17].

    See Also

    • Coma Cluster
    • Mass Estimation Methods

    References
    1. Biviano, A. (1998). "Subclustering in Coma." Proc. IAU.
    2. Kang, J., et al. (2025). "Substructures in Coma." ApJ.
    3. White, S. D. M., et al. (1993). "X-ray maps of Coma." Nature.
    4. Neumann, D. M., et al. (2001). "NGC 4839 group in X-rays." A&A.
    5. Baier, F. W., et al. (1990). "Projected galaxy distribution." Astron. Nachr.
    6. Biviano, A., et al. (1996). "NGC 4839 group dynamics." A&A.
    7. Lyskova, N., et al. (2019). "NGC 4839 secondary infall." MNRAS.
    8. Churazov, E., et al. (2021). "NGC 4839 infall dynamics." A&A.
    9. Adami, C., et al. (2005). "Coma merger history." A&A.
    10. Costa, A. P., et al. (2025). "Faint substructures in Coma." ApJ.
    11. Pedratti, S., et al. (2026). "Coma Cluster kinematics with DESI. I." arXiv:2603.16706
    12. Pizzuti, L., et al. (2020). "Gaussianity diagnostics." MNRAS.
    13. Roberts, I. D., et al. (2018). "Relaxation diagnostics." MNRAS.
    14. Ribeiro, A. L. B., et al. (2013). "Non-Gaussianity in disturbed systems." MNRAS.
    15. Anderson, T. W., Darling, D. A. (1952). "Asymptotic theory of statistics." Ann. Math. Stat.
    16. Caldwell, N., et al. (1993). "Post-starburst galaxies in Coma." AJ.
    17. Gavazzi, G., et al. (2010). "Post-starburst galaxies in Coma periphery." A&A. DOI: 10.1051/0004-6361/201014153
    Tags: Galaxy Clusters
